storyContext: The friend's tower fell down, and baby reaches over to help rebuild it - a tiny act of kindness that means the world
steps: Point out when a friend needs help
Discuss how helping made the friend feel

name: Family Game Night Simple
description: Play a very simple family game together, targeting family bonding and turn-taking. Teaches sharing, turn-taking, and emotional regulation for playing well with others.
storyContext: The whole family sits around the game, taking turns and laughing together - baby's first family game night is the best night ever
materials: simple toddler game like matching cards

name: Community Helper Recognition
description: Learn about helpers in the community, targeting social awareness and community. Teaches sharing, turn-taking, and emotional regulation for playing well with others.
storyContext: Firefighter, doctor, teacher - baby discovers all the special people who help the world go round
steps: Point out helpers in real life
Play pretend: be a doctor, firefighter
Visit a fire station if possible

name: Foam Soap Sensory Play
description: Play with foamy soap on a tray for tactile fun, targeting tactile exploration and creative play. Builds sensory discrimination for attention to detail and environmental comfort.
storyContext: Mountains of fluffy foam cover the tray and tiny hands mold it into clouds and castles of pure white softness
steps: Squirt foaming soap on a tray
Let toddler spread and mold it
Draw shapes and letters in foam

name: Scented Playdough Making
description: Make playdough together with different scents, targeting olfactory and cooking skills. Builds sensory discrimination for attention to detail and environmental comfort.
storyContext: Lavender dough smells like a garden, cinnamon dough like a kitchen - every color has its own special smell
steps: Add different scents to each batch

name: Gloop and Goo Experiment
description: Make cornstarch and water mixture for unique sensory experience, targeting unique texture and science. Builds sensory discrimination for attention to detail and environmental comfort.
storyContext: Is it a liquid? Is it a solid? The gloopy goo flows through fingers but freezes when squeezed - pure science magic
steps: Mix equal parts cornstarch and water
Notice it's solid when squeezed, liquid when relaxed

name: Sound Jar Matching
description: Match sealed jars by the sounds they make when shaken, targeting auditory matching and discrimination. Builds sensory discrimination for attention to detail and environmental comfort.
storyContext: Shake, shake, listen! This one sounds like the other one! Baby matches sounds like a detective following audio clues
materials: sealed jars in pairs with rice, beans, bells, coins
steps: Make pairs of sealed jars with same contents
Ask 'which other one sounds the same?'

name: Nature Collection Sensory Box
description: Collect nature items for a sensory exploration box, targeting nature awareness and texture variety. Builds sensory discrimination for attention to detail and environmental comfort.
storyContext: Pine cones, smooth stones, acorns, and shells - baby's nature box is a treasure chest of the outdoors brought inside
steps: Go on a nature walk with collection bag
Arrange in a special box at home

name: Frozen Dinosaur Excavation
description: Freeze small toys in ice for excavation play, targeting tactile and problem solving. Builds sensory discrimination for attention to detail and environmental comfort.
storyContext: Frozen in ice like ancient fossils, tiny dinosaurs wait to be freed by the warm water and determined little hands
steps: Freeze small toys in a container of water overnight
Give toddler warm water to pour on ice

name: Barefoot Outdoor Exploration
description: Walk barefoot on different outdoor surfaces, targeting proprioceptive input and texture awareness. Builds sensory discrimination for attention to detail and environmental comfort.
storyContext: Warm sand, cool grass, smooth pavement - every step brings a new surprise for bare adventuring feet
steps: Name each sensation: tickly, warm, smooth

name: DIY Musical Instruments Making
description: Make instruments from household items, targeting auditory creativity and construction. Builds sensory discrimination for attention to detail and environmental comfort.
storyContext: A rice shaker, a rubber band guitar, a pot drum - baby's homemade band is the loudest and most creative in town
steps: Make a shaker: rice in sealed container
Make a drum: pot with spoon
Make a guitar: rubber bands on box

name: Jumping with Both Feet
description: Practice jumping forward with both feet leaving the ground, targeting jumping and coordination. Strengthens muscles and coordination, supporting running, climbing, drawing, and self-care.
storyContext: Both feet leave the ground at the same time and for one amazing moment, baby is FLYING through the air!

name: Pedaling a Tricycle Independently
description: Ride a tricycle with actual pedaling, targeting pedaling and leg coordination. Strengthens muscles and coordination, supporting running, climbing, drawing, and self-care.
storyContext: Round and round go the pedals as tiny legs power the trike forward - baby is driving their very own vehicle

name: Catching a Bounced Ball
description: Catch a ball that's been bounced toward them, targeting catching and hand-eye coordination. Strengthens muscles and coordination, supporting running, climbing, drawing, and self-care.
storyContext: Bounce! The ball comes up and open arms wrap around it - the perfect catch that makes everyone cheer

name: Stringing Small Beads
description: Thread small beads onto a string for fine motor practice, targeting fine motor and concentration. Strengthens muscles and coordination, supporting running, climbing, drawing, and self-care.
storyContext: Tiny fingers thread one bead after another onto the string, creating a colorful bracelet of patience and precision
steps: Tie off to make a necklace or bracelet

name: Hopping on One Foot Attempts
description: Try hopping on one foot with support, targeting balance and single leg strength. Strengthens muscles and coordination, supporting running, climbing, drawing, and self-care.
storyContext: Balance on one foot and HOP! It's tricky and wobbly but each attempt gets a little bit higher and a little bit steadier

name: Drawing a Circle Shape
description: Practice drawing circles and closed shapes, targeting drawing and shape formation. Strengthens muscles and coordination, supporting running, climbing, drawing, and self-care.
storyContext: Round and round goes the crayon, and the line meets itself at the end - baby's first circle, a real shape drawn by real hands
steps: Draw a circle slowly for demonstration

name: Zipping and Buttoning Practice
description: Practice zipping zippers and buttoning large buttons, targeting self-dressing and fine motor. Strengthens muscles and coordination, supporting running, climbing, drawing, and self-care.
storyContext: Zip goes up, buttons click into place - baby is mastering the tiny mechanisms that hold clothes together
steps: Show how to align zipper base
Try large buttons on a frame
Be patient - this takes many tries

name: Simon Says Movement Game
description: Play Simon Says with increasingly complex movements, targeting body control and listening. Strengthens muscles and coordination, supporting running, climbing, drawing, and self-care.
storyContext: Simon says hop! Simon says spin! Following instructions and moving the body in brand new ways is the best game ever

name: Painting with a Brush
description: Use a real paint brush for more controlled art, targeting brush control and fine motor. Strengthens muscles and coordination, supporting running, climbing, drawing, and self-care.
storyContext: The brush dips in blue paint and sweeps across the paper - baby is a real artist now with a real brush in hand

name: Telling What Happened Today
description: Encourage child to recount their day's events, targeting narrative skills and memory. Develops sentence formation and conversational skills essential for social interaction.
storyContext: And then we went to the park, and I saw a bird, and we had a snack! Baby retells the whole day like a tiny storyteller
steps: At bedtime or dinner, ask about the day
What did you do at the park?
Help fill in details they miss

name: Why Questions Exploration
description: Engage with child's 'why' questions patiently, targeting questioning and critical thinking. Develops sentence formation and conversational skills essential for social interaction.
storyContext: Why is the sky blue? Why do birds fly? Every why is a window into baby's exploding curiosity about absolutely everything
steps: Turn questions back: 'why do YOU think?'

name: Letter Sound Introduction
description: Introduce the sounds that letters make, targeting phonics and letter awareness. Develops sentence formation and conversational skills essential for social interaction.
storyContext: Buh for B, sss for S - each letter has its own special sound, and baby is cracking the code of reading
steps: Start with letters in child's name
Find things starting with that sound
Focus on 1-2 letters at a time

name: I Spy Description Game
description: Play I Spy using colors, shapes, and descriptions, targeting descriptive language and observation. Develops sentence formation and conversational skills essential for social interaction.
storyContext: I spy with my little eye something... red and round! Baby scans the room, brain working, until they spot the apple!
steps: Let child do the spying too

name: Rhyming Book Exploration
description: Read rhyming books emphasizing the rhymes, targeting phonological awareness and rhyming. Develops sentence formation and conversational skills essential for social interaction.
storyContext: Cat and hat, bear and chair - the rhyming words bounce off each other like best friends playing a matching game
steps: Pause before the rhyme for child to guess
Try making up your own rhymes

name: Retelling a Favorite Story
description: Encourage child to retell a familiar story, targeting narrative sequencing and memory. Develops sentence formation and conversational skills essential for social interaction.
storyContext: Once upon a time... baby tells the story from memory, adding their own special twists and dramatic moments
steps: Ask 'can you tell me the story?'
Celebrate their version even if different

name: Conversation About Feelings
description: Have deeper conversations about emotions and causes, targeting emotional language and cause and effect. Develops sentence formation and conversational skills essential for social interaction.
storyContext: I was sad because my tower fell down. Baby is learning to explain not just what they feel, but why they feel it
steps: Ask 'how did that make you feel?'
Follow up: 'why did you feel sad?'
Share your own feelings and reasons
Use 'because' to connect cause and effect

name: Joke and Humor Sharing
description: Tell simple jokes and laugh together, targeting humor and social language. Develops sentence formation and conversational skills essential for social interaction.
storyContext: Knock knock! Who's there? Baby's first jokes might not make much sense, but the laughter they share is absolutely real
steps: Let child try to tell one
Silly word substitutions are great starters

name: Making Lists Together
description: Make simple lists: shopping list, things we'll do today, targeting functional language and planning. Develops sentence formation and conversational skills essential for social interaction.
storyContext: Apples, milk, bread - baby helps dictate the shopping list, each item a word captured on paper for the first time
steps: Ask 'what do we need from the store?'
Use the list at the store together

name: 5-Piece Puzzle Completion
description: Complete 5-6 piece puzzles independently, targeting spatial reasoning and problem solving. Builds counting, categorization, and problem-solving for structured learning readiness.
storyContext: Five pieces scattered on the table, and one by one they find their places until the beautiful picture appears
steps: Offer hints: 'look for the corner piece'

name: Counting Objects to 10
description: Count up to 10 objects accurately, targeting counting accuracy and one-to-one correspondence. Builds counting, categorization, and problem-solving for structured learning readiness.
storyContext: One, two, three... eight, nine, TEN! Baby counts all the way to ten with one careful finger touching each item

name: Color Mixing Experiment
description: Mix primary colors to discover new colors, targeting color theory and cause and effect. Builds counting, categorization, and problem-solving for structured learning readiness.
storyContext: Red and yellow make... ORANGE! Baby's eyes go wide with amazement at the magical science of color mixing
steps: Put red and yellow paint on tray
Mix together with finger or brush

name: Grouping Objects by Category
description: Sort objects into groups by category, targeting categorization and classification. Builds counting, categorization, and problem-solving for structured learning readiness.
storyContext: All the animals here, all the food here, all the vehicles there - baby is building the filing system for their brain

name: Building a Bridge with Blocks
description: Build a bridge structure with blocks, targeting engineering concepts and spatial reasoning. Builds counting, categorization, and problem-solving for structured learning readiness.
storyContext: Two towers with a block across the top - baby has built their first bridge and a toy car can drive right under it!
steps: Place a block across the top
Challenge child to build their own

name: First-Then Sequencing Cards
description: Arrange 2-3 picture cards in sequence, targeting temporal reasoning and sequencing. Builds counting, categorization, and problem-solving for structured learning readiness.
storyContext: First the egg, then the chick! Baby arranges the pictures in order, telling the story of what happens when

name: Shape Hunt Around the House
description: Find objects that match specific shapes, targeting shape recognition and real-world connection. Builds counting, categorization, and problem-solving for structured learning readiness.
storyContext: The clock is a circle! The book is a rectangle! Baby sees shapes hiding in every corner of the house
steps: Point out: 'the clock is a circle!'
Let child find their own examples

name: Memory Game with 4+ Pairs
description: Play memory matching with 4 or more pairs, targeting memory and concentration. Builds counting, categorization, and problem-solving for structured learning readiness.
storyContext: Flip, remember, match! Baby's memory gets stronger with each pair found, building a brain like a steel trap

name: Day and Night Concept Game
description: Discuss what happens during day vs night, targeting temporal concepts and categorization. Builds counting, categorization, and problem-solving for structured learning readiness.
storyContext: The sun comes up and we play! The moon comes out and we sleep! Baby understands the rhythm of days and nights
materials: pictures of day and night activities
steps: Ask 'what do we do at night?'

name: Cooperative Tower Building
description: Build a tall tower together taking turns, targeting cooperation and turn-taking. Teaches sharing, turn-taking, and emotional regulation for playing well with others.
storyContext: Your block, my block, your block, my block - together we build something taller and better than either could alone

name: Playing Doctor Check-Up
description: Set up a doctor play kit for empathy and role play, targeting empathy and caregiving. Teaches sharing, turn-taking, and emotional regulation for playing well with others.
storyContext: The stethoscope listens to teddy's heart, the bandage goes on the owie - baby doctor makes everyone feel better

name: Sharing Art Supplies
description: Practice sharing art supplies during creative activities, targeting sharing and cooperation. Teaches sharing, turn-taking, and emotional regulation for playing well with others.
storyContext: Can I have the red crayon? Here you go! Baby passes the crayon over, learning that sharing makes art more fun

name: Apologizing and Making Amends
description: Practice saying sorry and making things right, targeting apologizing and empathy. Teaches sharing, turn-taking, and emotional regulation for playing well with others.
storyContext: Sorry means I care about how you feel, and making amends means I want to fix it - the power of those small words
steps: Suggest amends: 'can you help fix it?'

name: Group Art Project
description: Create art together as a team, targeting collaboration and creative sharing. Teaches sharing, turn-taking, and emotional regulation for playing well with others.
storyContext: Everyone adds their part to the big picture - handprints, stickers, paint strokes - together making something beautiful
steps: Set up a large shared art surface

name: Pretend Restaurant Play
description: Set up a pretend restaurant for social role play, targeting role play and social scripting. Teaches sharing, turn-taking, and emotional regulation for playing well with others.
storyContext: Welcome to baby's restaurant! The little chef takes orders, cooks pretend food, and serves it with a big smile
steps: Take turns being chef and customer
Include cleanup as part of play

name: Making Friends at the Park
description: Support child in approaching and playing with other children, targeting social initiation and friendship skills. Teaches sharing, turn-taking, and emotional regulation for playing well with others.
storyContext: Hi! Want to play? Baby walks up to a new friend at the park and together they discover the slide and the swings
steps: Go to a park with other children

name: Turn-Taking Board Game
description: Play a simple board game with turn-taking rules, targeting turn-taking and rule following. Teaches sharing, turn-taking, and emotional regulation for playing well with others.
storyContext: Spin the wheel, move the piece, wait for your turn - baby's first board game teaches patience and fun in equal measure

name: Thank You Note Drawing
description: Draw pictures as thank you notes for gifts or kindnesses, targeting gratitude and thoughtfulness. Teaches sharing, turn-taking, and emotional regulation for playing well with others.
storyContext: Baby draws a picture just for grandma, a colorful thank-you that says I love you with crayons instead of words
steps: Help write or dictate a message

name: Play Foam Sculpting
description: Create shapes and figures with play foam, targeting tactile creativity and 3D forming. Builds sensory discrimination for attention to detail and environmental comfort.
storyContext: Squishy foam takes any shape baby's imagination can dream up - a snowman, a snake, a smiley face of pure creativity
materials: play foam or shaving cream mixed with cornstarch

name: Scented Sensory Bin
description: Create a sensory bin with herbs and scents, targeting olfactory exploration and multi-sensory. Builds sensory discrimination for attention to detail and environmental comfort.
storyContext: Rosemary, mint, and lavender hiding in the rice - every scoop brings a new smell to tiny exploring nostrils
steps: Add dried herbs to rice sensory bin

name: Obstacle Course with Sensory Stations
description: Create a course with different sensory experiences at each stop, targeting multi-sensory integration and motor planning. Builds sensory discrimination for attention to detail and environmental comfort.
storyContext: Crawl through the tunnel, stomp on the bubble wrap, dig in the sand, ring the bell - a sensory adventure course!
steps: Include: sand, water, bells, textures, bubbles

name: Musical Instrument Exploration
description: Explore real simple instruments: xylophone, recorder, tambourine, targeting auditory exploration and musical awareness. Builds sensory discrimination for attention to detail and environmental comfort.
storyContext: Each instrument makes its own special voice - the xylophone sings, the tambourine dances, and baby plays them all
steps: Present instruments one at a time

name: Taste Testing Game
description: Blind taste test different safe foods, targeting taste discrimination and vocabulary. Builds sensory discrimination for attention to detail and environmental comfort.
storyContext: Close your eyes and take a bite - is it sweet? Sour? Crunchy? Baby's tongue becomes a food detective
steps: Prepare small pieces of different foods

name: Shadow Play Theater
description: Use flashlight and hands to make shadow puppets, targeting visual exploration and creativity. Builds sensory discrimination for attention to detail and environmental comfort.
storyContext: A dog! A bird! A butterfly! Shadows dance on the wall as baby's fingers learn to shape the light into magical creatures

name: Texture Collage Art
description: Create a collage using different textured materials, targeting tactile creativity and art skills. Builds sensory discrimination for attention to detail and environmental comfort.
storyContext: Cotton balls, sandpaper, foil, and fabric all find a home on the collage - a touch-and-feel masterpiece
steps: Let child choose and place materials

name: Relaxation Sound Meditation
description: Listen to calming sounds for sensory regulation, targeting auditory calming and self-regulation. Builds sensory discrimination for attention to detail and environmental comfort.
storyContext: Lying on the floor with eyes closed, ocean waves and bird songs wash over baby, teaching the art of being still and calm
steps: Talk about what sounds you hear

name: Galloping Like a Horse
description: Practice galloping with one foot leading, targeting galloping and coordination. Develops advanced coordination for sports readiness and writing preparation.
storyContext: Clip-clop clip-clop! Little legs gallop across the yard like a wild pony running free through the fields

name: Scissor Cutting Along Lines
description: Cut along straight and curved lines with scissors, targeting scissor control and bilateral coordination. Develops advanced coordination for sports readiness and writing preparation.
storyContext: Snip snip along the line, tiny hands guiding the scissors with growing control and concentration
steps: Practice holding paper with other hand

name: Climbing Jungle Gym
description: Climb playground equipment with increasing skill, targeting climbing and upper body strength. Develops advanced coordination for sports readiness and writing preparation.
storyContext: Hands grip, feet find holds, and up goes the little climber conquering the jungle gym like a tiny mountaineer
steps: Spot child but let them climb

name: Catching with One Bounce
description: Catch a ball after one bounce consistently, targeting catching timing and hand-eye coordination. Develops advanced coordination for sports readiness and writing preparation.
storyContext: Bounce! Catch! The ball springs up and two hands trap it perfectly - timing and reflexes working together

name: Walking Balance Beam
description: Walk a low balance beam without stepping off, targeting balance and core strength. Develops advanced coordination for sports readiness and writing preparation.
storyContext: One foot carefully in front of the other, arms out for balance, crossing the beam like a tightrope walker in training
materials: low balance beam or taped line
steps: Start with tape line on floor

name: Drawing a Cross Shape
description: Practice drawing intersecting lines to form a cross, targeting drawing precision and line control. Develops advanced coordination for sports readiness and writing preparation.
storyContext: A vertical line meets a horizontal line in the middle, and baby has drawn a cross - a milestone in pencil control
steps: Draw a horizontal line crossing it

name: Building with Duplo Sets
description: Follow simple building instructions with large blocks, targeting following instructions and spatial reasoning. Develops advanced coordination for sports readiness and writing preparation.
storyContext: Following the picture step by step, colorful blocks click together into a car, a house, or an airplane

name: Hopscotch Introduction
description: Introduce basic hopscotch jumping patterns, targeting hopping and balance. Develops advanced coordination for sports readiness and writing preparation.
storyContext: One foot, two feet, one foot, two feet - hopping through the numbered squares is a game of balance and counting
materials: chalk or tape for hopscotch grid
steps: Draw or tape a simple hopscotch grid

name: Yoga Animal Poses
description: Practice simple yoga poses named after animals, targeting flexibility and balance. Develops advanced coordination for sports readiness and writing preparation.
storyContext: Stretch like a cat, stand like a tree, balance like a flamingo - yoga animals are the most peaceful game in the world
steps: Start with easy poses: cat stretch
Cobra pose: lying and lifting chest
Name the animal for each pose

name: Telling a Made-Up Story
description: Create an original story with beginning, middle, and end, targeting narrative creation and imagination. Strengthens grammar, narrative skills, and ability to express complex thoughts.
storyContext: Once upon a time, a little cat went on an adventure... baby's imagination weaves a story that's never been told before

name: Letter Recognition Games
description: Identify letters of the alphabet through play, targeting letter recognition and alphabet. Strengthens grammar, narrative skills, and ability to express complex thoughts.
storyContext: A, B, C! Baby spots the letters everywhere - on signs, on cereal boxes, in books - the alphabet is suddenly alive
steps: Start with letters in child's name

name: Complex Sentence Practice
description: Use and model sentences with 'because,' 'but,' and 'so', targeting complex grammar and conjunctions. Strengthens grammar, narrative skills, and ability to express complex thoughts.
storyContext: I can't go outside because it's raining - baby is using grown-up words to explain the because and the why of everything
steps: Model: 'I'm wearing a coat because it's cold'
When child gives reason, rephrase with 'because'

name: What Would Happen If...? Game
description: Ask hypothetical questions to spark imagination, targeting creative thinking and hypothesis. Strengthens grammar, narrative skills, and ability to express complex thoughts.
storyContext: What if dogs could fly? What if it rained candy? Baby's mind explodes with possibilities as imagination runs wild
steps: 'What would happen if fish could walk?'
Let child ask 'what if' questions too

name: Category Naming Race
description: Name as many things in a category as possible, targeting vocabulary retrieval and categorization. Strengthens grammar, narrative skills, and ability to express complex thoughts.
storyContext: Animals! Dog, cat, fish, bird, elephant! Baby's brain fires up listing everything they know in a category naming race
steps: Choose a category: animals, foods, colors
Count how many you both name

name: Show and Tell Practice
description: Practice presenting a favorite toy or object, targeting presentation skills and descriptive language. Strengthens grammar, narrative skills, and ability to express complex thoughts.
storyContext: This is my bear! He's brown and soft and sleeps with me every night - baby's first presentation to an adoring audience

name: Following Three-Step Directions
description: Give and follow three-step directions, targeting sequential processing and memory. Strengthens grammar, narrative skills, and ability to express complex thoughts.
storyContext: Get your shoes, put them by the door, and come back here - three instructions remembered and followed like a champion
steps: 'Pick up the book, put it on the shelf, then come sit down'
Help if they forget a step

name: Tongue Twister Fun
description: Try simple tongue twisters for speech clarity, targeting articulation and phonological awareness. Strengthens grammar, narrative skills, and ability to express complex thoughts.
storyContext: She sells seashells... wait, that's hard! Baby's tongue ties in funny knots trying to say the silly twisty words
steps: Let child make up their own

name: Puppet Dialog Creation
description: Create conversations between two puppets, targeting dialogue skills and perspective taking. Strengthens grammar, narrative skills, and ability to express complex thoughts.
storyContext: Hello bear! Hello bunny! The puppet friends chat away, making up stories and adventures with their own little voices
steps: Let child take over one puppet
Create scenarios for them to discuss

name: 8-12 Piece Puzzle Mastery
description: Complete 8-12 piece jigsaw puzzles, targeting spatial reasoning and persistence. Strengthens pattern recognition and logical sequencing needed for academic readiness.
storyContext: Twelve pieces become a complete picture as patient fingers find every match - puzzle master at work!

name: Counting to 20
description: Practice counting objects up to 20, targeting extended counting and number sequence. Strengthens pattern recognition and logical sequencing needed for academic readiness.
storyContext: Eleven, twelve, thirteen... all the way to twenty! The teen numbers are tricky but baby is mastering them all
steps: Use daily opportunities to count higher
Count stairs, steps, items at store

name: Simple Addition with Objects
description: Combine groups of objects and count the total, targeting early addition and number concepts. Strengthens pattern recognition and logical sequencing needed for academic readiness.
storyContext: Two blocks and two more blocks makes... one, two, three, FOUR blocks! Baby's first math equation solved with real things

name: Days of the Week Song
description: Learn the days of the week through song, targeting temporal concepts and sequencing. Strengthens pattern recognition and logical sequencing needed for academic readiness.
storyContext: Monday Tuesday Wednesday Thursday Friday Saturday Sunday! The days spin in a circle like a song that never ends
steps: Sing days of the week song daily
Connect to activities: 'Monday is swim day'

name: Comparing More vs Fewer
description: Compare groups and identify which has more or fewer, targeting comparison and quantity concepts. Strengthens pattern recognition and logical sequencing needed for academic readiness.
storyContext: This pile has MORE crackers and this pile has FEWER - baby is a math wizard comparing quantities with a single glance

name: Left and Right Introduction
description: Begin learning left and right directions, targeting directionality and body awareness. Strengthens pattern recognition and logical sequencing needed for academic readiness.
storyContext: Left hand wave! Right foot stomp! Baby starts sorting the world into two sides with new special direction words
steps: Put a sticker on child's right hand
Point out left and right in environment

name: First and Last Concept
description: Understand first and last in a sequence, targeting ordinal concepts and sequencing. Strengthens pattern recognition and logical sequencing needed for academic readiness.
storyContext: Who's first in line? Who's last? Baby understands that order matters and every position has a name
materials: toys or objects in a line
steps: Line up toys in a row
Apply to daily life: first step, last bite

name: Science Experiment: Sink or Float
description: Test which objects sink or float in water, targeting scientific thinking and prediction. Strengthens pattern recognition and logical sequencing needed for academic readiness.
storyContext: Will the rock float or sink? Down it goes! But the leaf floats on top! Baby is learning the secrets of water science

name: Emotion Matching Card Game
description: Match facial expressions to emotion words, targeting emotional recognition and vocabulary. Strengthens pattern recognition and logical sequencing needed for academic readiness.
storyContext: The happy face card matches the word HAPPY! Baby is reading emotions and connecting them to their names

name: Cooperative Game Playing
description: Play simple games that require cooperation not competition, targeting teamwork and shared goals. Develops teamwork and conflict resolution needed for thriving in group settings.
storyContext: Working together to rescue the princess from the dragon, baby and friends discover that teamwork wins every time
steps: Reinforce that helping each other is the goal

name: Conversation with Peers
description: Facilitate back-and-forth conversations with other children, targeting peer conversation and social language. Develops teamwork and conflict resolution needed for thriving in group settings.
storyContext: What's your favorite color? I like blue! Do you like dinosaurs? A real conversation between two tiny people is magical
steps: Set up play with a peer
Encourage: 'ask your friend what they like'

name: Understanding Others' Perspectives
description: Discuss how others might feel in different situations, targeting perspective taking and empathy. Develops teamwork and conflict resolution needed for thriving in group settings.
storyContext: How would you feel if someone took your toy? Baby starts to see the world through someone else's eyes

name: Following Game Rules
description: Learn and follow rules during structured games, targeting rule following and patience. Develops teamwork and conflict resolution needed for thriving in group settings.
storyContext: Wait your turn, roll the dice, move your piece - baby follows the rules and discovers that games are even more fun that way
steps: Remind of rules gently when needed
Discuss why rules make games fair

name: Friendship Skills Practice
description: Learn how to make and keep friends, targeting friendship skills and social competence. Develops teamwork and conflict resolution needed for thriving in group settings.
storyContext: Sharing, being kind, listening - baby learns the special recipe for making friends and keeping them close
steps: Practice: 'how do you ask someone to play?'

name: Waiting Patiently Activities
description: Practice waiting with specific strategies, targeting patience and self-regulation. Develops teamwork and conflict resolution needed for thriving in group settings.
storyContext: Waiting is hard, but counting to 10 or singing a song makes the time fly until it's finally your turn
steps: Teach waiting strategies: count, sing, fidget toy

name: Group Problem Solving
description: Work with peers to solve a simple problem, targeting group problem solving and communication. Develops teamwork and conflict resolution needed for thriving in group settings.
storyContext: How do we build a bridge that doesn't fall? Together, the tiny engineers brainstorm and try until they find the answer
steps: Present a challenge to the group

name: Cultural Food Sharing Day
description: Share foods from different cultures with friends, targeting cultural awareness and sharing. Develops teamwork and conflict resolution needed for thriving in group settings.
storyContext: Sushi, samosas, tacos, and pasta! Different families bring different foods, and baby tastes the flavors of the whole world
steps: Each family brings a cultural dish
Share the story behind the food

name: Kindness Chain Project
description: Create a paper chain with acts of kindness written on each link, targeting kindness and recognition. Develops teamwork and conflict resolution needed for thriving in group settings.
storyContext: Each colorful link in the chain is a kind thing baby did - soon the chain stretches across the whole room with love
steps: When child does something kind, write it down
Make a loop and connect to chain

name: Kinetic Sand Building
description: Build structures and shapes with kinetic sand, targeting tactile exploration and fine motor. Strengthens sensory integration for handwriting readiness and focused attention.
storyContext: Magical sand that sticks to itself but not to hands, forming castles and mountains that hold their shape like magic
steps: Give child kinetic sand on a tray

name: Rainbow Walking Discovery
description: Go on a walk looking for colors of the rainbow, targeting color recognition and observation. Strengthens sensory integration for handwriting readiness and focused attention.
storyContext: Red mailbox! Orange flower! Yellow sign! Baby hunts for every color of the rainbow on a colorful neighborhood walk
steps: Walk together looking for each color

name: Sound Story Creation
description: Create a story using sound effects, targeting auditory creativity and narrative. Strengthens sensory integration for handwriting readiness and focused attention.
storyContext: The rain goes pitter-patter, the door goes CREAK, and the cat goes meow - every sound tells part of the adventure story
steps: Add sound effects: knock on wood for door

storyContext: Soft fur meets tiny fingers as baby gently touches the family dog for the very first time, eyes wide with wonder
steps: Hold baby securely near calm pet

name: Two-Toy Play Switching
description: Give baby two toys and watch them switch attention, targeting attention switching and bilateral play. Develops early problem-solving and object awareness through hands-on exploration.
storyContext: One toy in each hand, baby looks left then right, deciding which deserves attention, before trading hands like a juggler
steps: Give baby a toy in each hand
Notice if they drop one to focus on other

name: Upside-Down Face Game
description: Lean over baby showing your face upside down, targeting visual novelty and social engagement. Builds emotional security and teaches turn-taking, the foundation of all social relationships.
storyContext: An upside-down face appears above and baby stares at this funny version of mama's face with delighted confusion
steps: Lean over baby so your face appears upside-down
This shows they recognize face orientation

name: Simple Sorting by Shape
description: Give baby round and square objects to explore, targeting shape awareness and tactile. Develops early problem-solving and object awareness through hands-on exploration.
storyContext: Round ball, square block - two shapes, two ways the world comes, and baby's hands learn the difference by feel
steps: Give baby a ball to explore
Let them hold and feel differences
This is shape awareness, not sorting yet

name: Water Squeeze Toy Bath
description: Give baby squeeze toys in the bath for cause-effect play, targeting cause and effect and hand strength. Encourages multi-sensory exploration that rapidly builds neural connections.
storyContext: Squeeze the rubber duck and water squirts out like a tiny fountain - baby discovers hydraulic engineering in the bathtub!
steps: Give baby a squeeze toy in the bath
Show squeezing to fill with water
The water shooting out is exciting

name: Supported Bouncing on Feet
description: Hold baby standing and let them bounce on their feet, targeting leg springs and weight bearing. Strengthens core muscles and coordination, preparing for sitting and reaching independently.
storyContext: Boing boing boing! Tiny feet discover the spring in their legs, bouncing with increasing enthusiasm and power
steps: Let feet touch a firm surface

name: Crawl Tunnel from Blankets
description: Drape blankets over chairs for a crawling tunnel, targeting crawling and spatial. Strengthens core muscles and coordination, preparing for sitting and reaching independently.
storyContext: Into the blanket tunnel baby crawls, discovering that dark cozy spaces lead to bright exciting ends
steps: Call baby from the other end
Make it part of an obstacle course

name: Toy Drop and Retrieve
description: Let baby drop toys from highchair and pick them up, targeting gravity and cause effect. Develops early problem-solving and object awareness through hands-on exploration.
storyContext: Down goes the toy and baby looks over the edge - then mama picks it up and it goes down again! Science is fascinating
steps: Let baby drop toys from highchair
This is gravity exploration, not misbehavior

name: Signing for More
description: Teach baby sign for 'more' during meals, targeting sign language and communication. Encourages babbling and sound exploration, building foundation for first words.
storyContext: Little hands tap together - the sign for MORE - and suddenly baby can tell you they want seconds without any words
steps: During meals, show the 'more' sign (fingertips tapping)
Always give food after the sign

name: First Puzzle Ring
description: Put a single ring on a peg, targeting spatial reasoning and fine motor. Strengthens memory, attention, and cause-effect understanding through interactive play.
storyContext: The ring slides down onto the peg and baby has solved their very first put-it-on puzzle - a proud moment!
steps: Add more rings as they master one

name: Stair Step Climbing Practice
description: Practice climbing up one or two steps safely, targeting climbing and leg strength. Develops balance and muscle control needed for crawling, standing, and first steps.
storyContext: One step up, two steps up! Baby conquers the stairs one careful step at a time with a spotter close behind
steps: Stand behind baby at bottom of stairs
Let them reach up to next step
Practice going up only at first

name: Signing All Done
description: Teach sign for 'all done' after meals and activities, targeting sign language and communication. Supports word recognition and early vocabulary, connecting words with objects and actions.
storyContext: Hands wave back and forth - ALL DONE! Baby's sign says 'I'm finished!' without needing any words at all
steps: At end of meals, wave hands for 'all done'
Use at end of activities too

name: Walking Behind a Chair
description: Push a dining chair across the floor for walking support, targeting walking and pushing. Develops balance and muscle control needed for crawling, standing, and first steps.
storyContext: A heavy dining chair becomes baby's personal walker as they push it across the kitchen floor with determined steps
steps: Position baby behind a dining chair

name: Walking Backward Steps
description: Practice taking steps backward, targeting backward walking and balance. Refines walking coordination and hand skills, building confidence for active exploration.
storyContext: Step back, step back - moving in reverse is a whole new brain challenge that makes walking even more interesting
steps: Try just a few steps at first
Let go of one hand as they improve

name: Where Is Thumbkin? Finger Song
description: Sing the finger family song with hand movements, targeting finger awareness and song. Expands vocabulary and encourages word combinations, reducing communication frustration.
storyContext: Where is thumbkin? Here I am! Each finger gets its own verse, its own name, and its own wiggly performance
steps: Hold up each finger in turn
This teaches finger names and individuation

name: Shape Tracing in Sand
description: Use finger to trace shapes in sand or salt tray, targeting shape formation and pre-writing. Refines walking coordination and hand skills, building confidence for active exploration.
storyContext: A finger draws circles and lines in the smooth sand, leaving trails of shapes that can be smoothed away and redrawn
steps: Fill a tray with sand or salt
Show drawing a circle with finger
Shake tray to erase and start fresh

name: Washing Hands Song Routine
description: Sing a hand washing song during every hand wash, targeting hygiene and routine. Nurtures empathy and self-awareness, introducing basics of cooperative play.
storyContext: Scrub scrub scrub to the special song! Twenty seconds of singing makes clean hands fun for everyone
steps: Sing every time hands are washed
Make it the same song always
Duration: whole song = proper washing time

name: Matching Shoes to Feet
description: Practice putting shoes on the correct feet, targeting left-right awareness and self-dressing. Builds counting, categorization, and problem-solving for structured learning readiness.
storyContext: Left shoe on left foot, right shoe on right foot - it's tricky but baby is learning which foot likes which shoe
steps: Point out which foot each fits
Let toddler try on their own

name: Stacking Block Patterns
description: Build specific color patterns with stacking blocks, targeting pattern building and color recognition. Builds counting, categorization, and problem-solving for structured learning readiness.
storyContext: Red blue red blue - baby follows the pattern and builds a tower of perfectly alternating colors

name: Animal Walking Game
description: Walk like different animals: waddle like a duck, hop like a bunny, targeting creative movement and imitation. Strengthens muscles and coordination, supporting running, climbing, drawing, and self-care.
storyContext: Waddle waddle like a duck! Hop hop like a bunny! Baby moves their body in brand new ways copying each animal
steps: Call out an animal: 'walk like a duck!'
Bear walking on hands and feet

name: Building with Magna-Tiles
description: Explore magnetic building tiles for creative construction, targeting spatial reasoning and magnetism. Builds counting, categorization, and problem-solving for structured learning readiness.
storyContext: Click! The colorful tiles snap together magnetically, building walls and shapes that hold by invisible force

name: Throwing Bean Bags at Targets
description: Toss bean bags at ground targets, targeting throwing accuracy and hand-eye. Strengthens muscles and coordination, supporting running, climbing, drawing, and self-care.
storyContext: One, two, three, THROW! The bean bag sails through the air and lands right on the target - bullseye!

name: Making Paper Airplanes
description: Fold and fly simple paper airplanes, targeting folding and throwing. Strengthens muscles and coordination, supporting running, climbing, drawing, and self-care.
storyContext: Fold fold fold and the paper transforms into a flying machine that soars across the room when baby gives it a throw

name: Emotion Role-Playing
description: Act out different emotions with body and face, targeting emotional expression and body language. Teaches sharing, turn-taking, and emotional regulation for playing well with others.
storyContext: Show me happy! Show me sad! Show me surprised! Baby puts on each feeling like a costume, mastering the art of expression

Model the facial expression and body language
Try: happy, sad, angry, scared, surprised

name: Threading Pasta Necklace
description: Thread dried pasta onto string for a wearable craft, targeting fine motor and threading. Develops advanced coordination for sports readiness and writing preparation.
storyContext: Piece by piece the pasta slides onto the string, making a necklace that jingles with every step baby takes
steps: Use large pasta with big holes
Tape end of string to make stiff
Tie ends to make a necklace

name: Traffic Light Game
description: Run on green, walk on yellow, stop on red, targeting body control and color recognition. Develops advanced coordination for sports readiness and writing preparation.
storyContext: GREEN means GO! YELLOW means slow down! RED means STOP! Baby runs, walks, and freezes in the traffic light game
materials: red, yellow, green cards or objects
steps: Let child hold the cards sometimes

name: Sensory Letter Writing
description: Write letters in shaving cream, sand, or finger paint, targeting letter formation and tactile learning. Develops advanced coordination for sports readiness and writing preparation.
storyContext: A big letter A drawn in foamy shaving cream - learning to write is way more fun when it's squishy and messy

name: Sorting Coins by Size
description: Sort coins into groups by their size, targeting size sorting and math foundations. Strengthens pattern recognition and logical sequencing needed for academic readiness.
storyContext: Big coins, medium coins, and tiny coins each find their group - baby sorts money like a little banker
steps: Big ones here, small ones there
This is a precursor to money understanding

name: Walking Heel to Toe
description: Practice walking with heel touching toe in a line, targeting balance and coordination. Develops advanced coordination for sports readiness and writing preparation.
storyContext: Heel to toe, heel to toe, walking a straight line like a tightrope walker with perfect focus and balance

name: Retelling Weekend Events on Monday
description: Recall and share what happened over the weekend, targeting recall and narrative. Strengthens grammar, narrative skills, and ability to express complex thoughts.
storyContext: On Saturday we went to the zoo and saw a giraffe! Baby retells the weekend adventures like a news reporter
steps: On Monday, ask about the weekend
This strengthens memory and narrative skills

name: Creating a Bug Hotel
description: Build a simple bug hotel from natural materials, targeting nature and construction. Strengthens pattern recognition and logical sequencing needed for academic readiness.
storyContext: Sticks, leaves, and pine cones stacked in a box make a cozy hotel for bugs, and baby is the architect-innkeeper
steps: Stack in a container or box
Leave gaps for bugs to enter

name: Treasure Map Drawing and Following
description: Draw a treasure map and follow it to find hidden treasure, targeting map reading and spatial reasoning. Strengthens pattern recognition and logical sequencing needed for academic readiness.
storyContext: X marks the spot! Baby follows the hand-drawn map through the house to find the hidden treasure at the end

Draw a simple map showing the path

name: Playing I Have, Who Has? Card Game
description: Play a simple verbal chain game with picture cards, targeting reading and listening. Strengthens grammar, narrative skills, and ability to express complex thoughts.
storyContext: I have a cat! Who has a dog? Baby reads their card and waits for the next player to continue the chain
steps: First player reads: 'I have a cat, who has a dog?'
Person with dog card goes next
Chain continues until all cards played

name: Drawing Self-Portraits
description: Draw a picture of themselves, targeting self-representation and fine motor. Builds refined physical control needed for handwriting, sports, and school-ready independence.
storyContext: Brown hair, blue eyes, a big smile - baby draws a picture of themselves, a self-portrait that captures exactly how they see the world

name: Reading Chapter Book Chapters
description: Start reading simple chapter books together, targeting extended narrative and memory. Builds literacy readiness through phonological awareness and storytelling ability.
storyContext: Chapter 1, chapter 2, chapter 3 - the story stretches across days and baby remembers where they left off each night
steps: This builds narrative memory and attention span

name: Creating a Weather Journal
description: Record daily weather observations in a journal, targeting observation and record keeping. Develops mathematical thinking and critical reasoning skills needed for school success.
storyContext: Sunny with clouds today! Baby draws the weather and writes the temperature, keeping their very own weather diary
steps: Draw a picture of the sky
Write or draw: sunny, rainy, cloudy

name: Friendship Bracelet Making
description: Create simple bracelets to give to friends, targeting fine motor and friendship. Builds leadership, cooperation, and emotional intelligence for school and friendships.
storyContext: Beads slide onto the string in a pattern chosen with care, a handmade gift that says 'you are my friend' without words

name: Adding and Subtracting with Fingers
description: Use fingers to solve simple math problems, targeting finger math and addition. Develops mathematical thinking and critical reasoning skills needed for school success.
storyContext: Hold up 3 fingers, add 2 more, count all fingers - FIVE! Baby's hands are the best calculator ever invented
steps: Try subtraction: start with 5, put 2 down
Make it a game with different numbers

name: Creating Stop Motion Animation
description: Make a simple stop-motion video with toys, targeting technology and patience. Develops mathematical thinking and critical reasoning skills needed for school success.
storyContext: Move the toy, take a photo, move it again, take another - when played fast, the toy comes to life like real movie magic!
steps: Play all photos fast - the toy moves!

name: Learning About Recycling
description: Sort waste into recycling categories, targeting environmental awareness and sorting. Builds leadership, cooperation, and emotional intelligence for school and friendships.
storyContext: Paper here, plastic there, compost in this bin - baby learns that sorting trash helps take care of the whole planet
steps: Make it part of daily routine

name: Cooking Measurement Practice
description: Measure ingredients using cups and spoons, targeting measurement and math. Develops mathematical thinking and critical reasoning skills needed for school success.
storyContext: One cup of flour, half a teaspoon of salt - baby measures ingredients with growing precision, a mini scientist in the kitchen

Let child scoop and level flour
Use measuring spoons for small amounts
Connect to fractions: half cup, quarter cup

name: Soft Fabric Brush on Cheek
description: Brush soft fabric gently across baby's cheek, targeting tactile sensitivity and facial awareness. Activates sensory pathways, helping your baby process sights, sounds, and textures.
storyContext: The softest silk whispers across baby's cheek like a butterfly's kiss, and tiny eyes flutter at the gentle sensation

name: Cooing Encouragement
description: Respond enthusiastically to every coo and sound baby makes, targeting vocalization encouragement and communication. Stimulates early language pathways and teaches that sounds are meaningful communication.
storyContext: Every tiny coo gets an excited response, teaching baby that their voice matters and communication is powerful
steps: When baby coos, smile big and respond
Never ignore baby's attempts to communicate
This is the foundation of all language

name: Parent Voice Reading
description: Read anything aloud - newspaper, recipe, book - for voice exposure, targeting language exposure and voice familiarity. Stimulates early language pathways and teaches that sounds are meaningful communication.
storyContext: The sound of mama reading a recipe aloud is just as wonderful as any storybook when you're learning the music of words
steps: The content doesn't matter at this age
It's your voice and language patterns that matter
Read during feeding or floor time

name: Hand Discovery Watching
description: Let baby discover and study their own hands, targeting hand awareness and visual focus. Stimulates brain development through sensory experience, building cause-and-effect understanding.
storyContext: Baby raises their hands and stares in wonder - whose hands are those? The most fascinating toys are attached to their own body
steps: Watch as they discover their hands
They'll stare intently at their fingers

name: Patterned Sound Making
description: Create rhythmic patterns with sounds near baby, targeting pattern detection and auditory processing. Activates sensory pathways, helping your baby process sights, sounds, and textures.
storyContext: Tap tap tap... pause... tap tap tap - baby's brain detects the pattern in the sounds, even at just a few weeks old

name: Toy Shake Encouragement
description: When baby holds a rattle, encourage intentional shaking, targeting intentional movement and cause and effect. Builds early muscle strength and body awareness needed for head control and rolling.
storyContext: Baby's hand grips the rattle and gives a deliberate shake - the first time they make something happen completely on purpose

name: Cloth Peek-a-Boo on Baby's Face
description: Place lightweight cloth on baby's face for them to pull off, targeting problem solving and hand control. Stimulates brain development through sensory experience, building cause-and-effect understanding.
storyContext: A thin cloth lands softly on baby's face and - pull! - tiny hands sweep it away, revealing the world again
steps: Place a thin cloth lightly on baby's face
Wait for baby to pull it off
If baby gets frustrated, remove quickly

name: Social Smile Interactions
description: Engage face-to-face to elicit social smiles, targeting social smiling and bonding. Strengthens parent-child bond, teaching that social interaction is safe and rewarding.
storyContext: The first real social smile - not gas, not reflex, but a genuine smile meant just for you - is the most magical moment
steps: Wait for baby to smile back
This is different from reflexive smiling

name: Overhead Toy Batting
description: Hang toys for baby to bat at from lying position, targeting arm coordination and visual-motor. Builds early muscle strength and body awareness needed for head control and rolling.
storyContext: Arms swing upward and - contact! The hanging toy swings, and baby learns that their arms can make things move

name: Response to Name
description: Say baby's name frequently in different contexts, targeting name recognition and attention. Stimulates early language pathways and teaches that sounds are meaningful communication.
storyContext: Baby's name is the most important word in the whole language, and hearing it over and over helps them know it's theirs
steps: Say it at the start of interactions
Watch for head turning toward name

name: Independent Sitting Practice
description: Let baby practice sitting without support briefly, targeting independent sitting and core balance. Strengthens core muscles and coordination, preparing for sitting and reaching independently.
storyContext: Wobble, steady, wobble, BALANCE! Baby sits alone for a few precious seconds before toppling gently sideways

name: Transferring Objects Between Hands
description: Watch and encourage baby to move objects from hand to hand, targeting bilateral transfer and coordination. Strengthens core muscles and coordination, preparing for sitting and reaching independently.
storyContext: From left to right and back again, the toy travels between baby's hands like a precious package being examined from all angles
steps: Give baby a toy in one hand
Watch if they transfer to other hand
This is a key motor milestone

name: Simple Gesture Games
description: Teach clap hands and arms-up gestures, targeting gesture imitation and social play. Builds emotional security and teaches turn-taking, the foundation of all social relationships.
storyContext: Arms up high! Clap your hands! Baby joins the gesture game, learning that movements can be shared and celebrated

name: Vocal Turn-Taking Games
description: Have back-and-forth vocal exchanges, targeting turn-taking and vocalization. Encourages babbling and sound exploration, building foundation for first words.

name: Rattle Shaking to Music
description: Shake a rattle in time with music, targeting rhythm and auditory-motor. Encourages multi-sensory exploration that rapidly builds neural connections.
storyContext: Shake shake shake to the beat! Baby's rattle keeps time with the music like a tiny percussionist in training
steps: Play music with a clear beat
Let them shake however they like

name: Banging Two Objects Together
description: Give baby two objects to bang together, targeting bilateral banging and coordination. Strengthens core muscles and coordination, preparing for sitting and reaching independently.
storyContext: CLANG! Two blocks crash together in tiny hands, making the most satisfying sound a 7-month-old has ever created
steps: Give baby a block in each hand

name: Stranger Wariness Support
description: Help baby feel safe when meeting new people, targeting stranger anxiety and trust. Builds emotional security and teaches turn-taking, the foundation of all social relationships.
storyContext: A new face approaches and baby buries their face in mama's shoulder - stranger wariness means baby knows their people
steps: Let baby warm up on their own timeline
Stranger wariness is healthy and normal

name: Object Exploration: Mouthing Phase
description: Provide safe objects for oral exploration, targeting oral exploration and object properties. Encourages multi-sensory exploration that rapidly builds neural connections.
storyContext: Everything goes to the mouth! Baby explores each object's taste, texture, and temperature with their most sensitive sensor
steps: Mouthing is primary exploration at this age

name: Sitting and Playing Independently
description: Let baby sit and play with toys without direct help, targeting independent play and focus. Develops early problem-solving and object awareness through hands-on exploration.
storyContext: Sitting independently among scattered toys, baby picks up, examines, and discards each one like a tiny scientist at work

name: Waving at Reflections
description: Encourage baby to wave at their reflection in mirrors, targeting self-awareness and social gesture. Builds emotional security and teaches turn-taking, the foundation of all social relationships.
storyContext: Baby waves at the mirror baby and the mirror baby waves back - the most reliable friend who always copies everything

name: Cruising Between Objects

Watch if they bring it to their head
Try: drink from cup, talk on phone

name: Offering Food to Others
description: Encourage baby to share food by offering pieces, targeting sharing foundations and generosity. Develops shared attention and imitation skills for learning through social engagement.

name: Water Cup Pouring in Bath
description: Pour water between cups in the bathtub, targeting pouring and hand control. Develops sensory integration, helping coordinate what baby sees, hears, and feels.
storyContext: From one cup to the other, water cascades and splashes in the warm bath - a tiny waterfall factory at work
steps: Give baby cups in the bath
From big cup to small, small to big
The spillage is the fun part

name: Pointing at Book Pictures
description: Point at pictures in books and name them, targeting pointing and word learning. Supports word recognition and early vocabulary, connecting words with objects and actions.
storyContext: Baby's finger lands on the cat picture and mama says 'cat!' - every point earns a new word in baby's growing vocabulary

name: Walking and Squatting to Pick Up

storyContext: Walking along, baby spots a toy, squats down gracefully, picks it up, and keeps on walking - multitasking achieved!

Standing back up is the challenge

Point to objects as you name them

Let toddler call parts for you too

name: Pretend Ice Cream Shop

name: Nature Sorting: Leaves vs Sticks

Back home, sort into two groups
Try other categories: big vs small

name: Learning Phone Number Song

name: Color Scavenger Hunt

Try a different color next time

name: Texture Painting with Sponges

Create a pattern or free design

name: Setting the Table Helper

Let them place it on the table

Let child trace around the shadow
Come back later to see shadows moved

name: Remembering and Reciting Poems
description: Memorize and recite simple poems, targeting memory and recitation. Strengthens grammar, narrative skills, and ability to express complex thoughts.

Try a new poem when one is mastered
